title: A user-friendly heavy duty dual-hooks picture frame hanger
abstract: Present invention discloses a user-friendly heavy duty dual-hooks plastic or metal hanger that has a hook-above-nail arrangement, and load capacity and structural strength equivalent to that of conventional nail-above-hook metal hanger.
claims:
Claims 1. A heavy duty dual-hooks hanger comprising of a base plate, and an identical first and second lateral projectiles integrally connected to said base plate to form an identical left and right hook bodies of said dual-hooks hanger, in which: said base plate has a left, central and right rectangular sections, said left or right section has a height shorter than the height of said central section, and said left and right hook bodies are integrally connected to said left and right sections of said base plate respectively; said first or second lateral projectile comprises of a lower body having a substantially uniform lateral width, a transition body projecting upwardly and forwardly from said lower body, and an upper body projecting upwardly from said transition body; said transition body has an inclined open top surface between the top edge of the rear surface of said transition body and the bottom edge of the rear surface of said upper body, said open top surface has a lateral width determined by a horizontal distance between said edges, and said open top surface forms the internal side of bottom closed end of said left or right hook body for retaining a hanging wire of a picture frame; said first or second lateral projectile has a continuous front surface formed by the front surfaces of said lower, transition and upper bodies, in which, said front surface has a first horizontal juncture edge between said front surfaces of said lower and transition bodies, and a second horizontal juncture edge between said front surfaces of said upper and transition bodies; said first juncture edge is separated from said rear surface of said transition body by a first horizontal distance equal to or greater than said lateral width of said open top surface of said transition body, and separated from said top edge of said rear surface of said transition body by a first vertical distance equal to or greater than two times of said lateral width of said open top surface of said transition body; said second juncture edge is separated from said first juncture edge by a vertical distance greater than said first vertical distance; and said second juncture edge is separated from said rear surface of said transition body by a horizontal distance equal to or greater than two times of said lateral width of said open top surface of said transition body; said transition body has an incline nail hole for guiding a nail penetrating across said transition body and continuously penetrating across said base plate, and said transition body 

 has a nail hole exit located at said rear surface of said transition body immediately below said top edge of said rear surface; and said nail hole exit has a height above the bottom edge of said lower body that is greater than four times of said lateral width of said open top surface of said transition body. 2. The heavy duty dual-hooks hanger according to claim 1, in which, said incline nail hole or said open top surface of said transition body has an incline angle of 15Â° projecting upwardly and forwardly. 3. The heavy duty dual -hooks hanger according to claim 1, in which, said first or second lateral projectile is integrally connected to said base plate by abutting the rear surface of said lower body and said rear surface of said transition body against said base plate, having said bottom edge of said lower body flush to the bottom edge of said base plate, and having said top edge of said rear surface of said transition body flush to the top edge of said left or right section of said base plate; said central section of said base plate has a upper and lower central sections above and below said height of said left or right section of said base plate respectively; said lower central section has a wall thickness slightly greater the wall thickness of said upper central section; a notch is provided at the top edge of said upper central section for indicating the center location of said hanger; and the top edge of said lower central section provides means for indicating the elevation of said internal side of bottom closed end of said left or right hook body. 4. The heavy duty dual-hooks hanger according to claim 1, in which, a third lateral projectile is integrally and centrally connected to said central section of said base plate, and said third lateral projectile is capable of making a contact with said hanging wire of picture frame and providing means for restricting slippage of said hanging wire within said left and right hook bodies. 5. The heavy duty dual -hooks hanger according to claim 1, in which, a third lateral projectile is integrally and centrally connected to said central section of said base plate, and has an inclined nail hole for guiding a nail penetrating across downwardly from the front surface to the rear surface of said third lateral projectile, and continuously penetrating across said base plate; and 

 said inclined nail hole in said third lateral projectile has a 15Â° incline angle identical to that of said inclined nail hole in said first or second lateral projectile.
